title: Eulalie by Edgar Allan Poe — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
I dwelt alone
             In a world of moan,
         And my soul was a stagnant tide,
Till the fair and gentle Eulalie became my blushing bride--
Till the yellow-haired young Eulalie became my smiling bride.
             Ah, less--less bright
             The stars of the night
         Than the eyes of the radiant girl!
meaning: "Eulalie" by Edgar Allan Poe is a sad and love and nature poem consisting of 22 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "             I dwelt alone              In a world of moan,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edgar Allan Poe's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
